reasoning: The featured_japan_heritage_food_stories block fires story_id 051 which is precisely about 中芸地域 yuzu (covering the five correct municipalities: Naharicho, Tanocho, Yasudacho, Kitagawamura, Umajimura) — this is authoritative and highly relevant. However, the items array surfaces 物部ゆず (a different MAFF GI from Kami City, not 中芸地域), and the scraped_local_food entries are severe noise (a Buddhist temple labeled regional_dish, a generic travel itinerary page). 中芸ゆず's own GI registration is not surfaced in the visible 500 items despite being the explicit query target. RULE C applies: authoritative data is present but scope/filter gap exists — prefecture-wide call with no keyword narrowing buried the specific GI. RULE B: one root cause (no keyword/sub-region filter → ranking noise dominates items).
missing_data: 中芸ゆず MAFF GI registration (distinct from 物部ゆず) is absent from visible results. Production figures, harvest season, processing methods (ponzu, yuzu kosho), and visitor access to ゆずロード are not present in the items.
wrong_data: 物部ゆず (Monobe yuzu, Kami City) is surfaced as the primary GI item but is a different sub-regional brand from a different municipality — not 中芸ゆず. Scraped items include 日蓮宗 要法寺 (a Buddhist temple) categorized as regional_dish, which is a data classification error.
improvement_hint: Add keyword filtering: when the query contains a specific GI name or sub-region toponym (中芸, 物部, 馬路 etc.), filter items by matching name_ja/description_ja/production_area_text before returning. Also fix scraped_local_food category assignment — non-food spots (temples, itinerary pages) should not appear in regional_dish category.
